content.full_text:
Farn Pourrcal PnacrICES CorrautssloN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56@ . Fax (916) 322-0886
August 5,2404
Mark C. Joseph
Strawberry Recreation Di stri ct
1 18 East Strawberry Drive
Mill Valley, CA 94941-2594
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. l-04-167
Dear Mr. Joseph
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the Strawberry Recreation
District for advice regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
'Act').r Please note that nothing in this letter is intended to address the propriety of any
past mailings, as we do not render advice regarding past conduct. In light ofthis fact and
the fact that we do not have sufficient informafion to provide you with specific advice,
we are providing general advice and are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.2
QUESTION
May a brochure produced by the Strawberry Recreation District ("District")
include a letter from the chair of the District's board of directors?
CONCLUSION
You have not provided sufficient information for us to determine whether the
brochure is subject to the Act's prohibition on mass mailings at public expense in section
89001. However, the analysis below will assist you in making that determination.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Regulation 1 8329(c).)
=.-{ird


File No. I-04-167
Page No. 2
FACTS
Brochures for the Strawberry Recreation District are published three times ayear.
The brochure describes your programs, classes, camps and activities at the recreation
district. You plan to include in the brochure a letter from the chairman of the board of
directors. The directors are elected by the public.
ANALYSIS
Section 89001 of the Act provides that "[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing
shall be sent at public expense." Under section 820415 and Commission regulations
18435 and 18901, a mass mailing is defined as more than 200 substantially similar
tangible items delivered in a calendar month, by any means, to recipients at their
residence, place of employment, business, or post oflice box. Regulation 18901(a) states:
"(a) Except as provided in subdivision O), a mailing is
prohibited by section 89001 if all of the following criteria
are met:
(l) AnV item sent is delivered,by any means, to the
recipient at his or her residence, place of employment or
business, or post office box. For purposes of this
subdivision (aXl), the item delivered to the recipient must
be a tangible item, such as a videotape, record, or button, or
a written document.
Q) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other
reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency
which produces or sends the mailing, and is prepared or
sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert
with the elected officer.
(3XA) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with
public moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding
$50.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items
are sent, in a single calendar month, excluding any item
sent in response to an unsolicited request and any item
described in subdivision (b)."
In order for the mass mailing provisions to apply, all four of the criteria listed above must
be met.


File No. I-04-167
Page No. 3
Transmission
Regulation 18901(a)(1) only restricts items that are mailed or delivered, by any
me,uls, to a person's home, office or post office box. If items are set out for the public to
pick up on their own, or are handed out in a public area, the restrictions of the regulation
do not apply. You have not described how the brochures are distributed. Ifthe brochures
are mailed or delivered, by any means, to a person's home, office or post office box, this
criterion would be met.
Inclusion of a Reference to an Elected Oflicer
A mass mailing which does not include the name, photograph or anyreference to
an elected officer who is affiliated with the agency that produces or distributes the
mailing is not subject to the restrictions of the regulation. (Regulation 18901(a)(2).) An
elected officer is "affiliated" with an agency if the officer is a "member, officer, or
employee of the agency, or of a subunit thereof such as a cofltmittee, or who has
supervisory control over the agency, or who appoints one or more members of the
agency;' (Regulation 18901(c)(l).) You state that the Dstrict's directors are elected
officials. Therefore, the chair of the board of directors is an elected official affiliated
with the agencyproducing the brochure.
Moreover, if the brochure is prepared or sent in "cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with the elected officer," any use of the elected officer's name,
photograph or office, or any reference to the officer is prohibited. (Regulation
18e01(a)(2XB).)
You wish to include in the brochure a message with the heading "Chair's Letter
by Richard Rubin." Under such circumstances, the content of the brochure will be
prepared in "cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert" with an elected official.
Accordingly, the brochure may not include the chair's name orphotograph or any
reference to him or his office unless one or more of the other three criteria is not met or a
specific exception applies. (Dale Advice Letter, No. 4-02-331.)
Public Moneys
Pursuant to regulation 18901(a)(3XA) and (B), if "[a]ny of the costs of
distribution are paid for with public moneys; or [c]osts of design, production, and
printing exceeding 550.00 are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as permitted by this
regulation," the criterion will be met. You note that the brochure is published by the
Strawberry Recreation District. We assume for purposes of this letter that regulation
18901(a)(3)(A) is met.


File No. I-04-167
Page No. 4
Number Distributed
We assume again forpurposes ofproviding advice that more than 200 brochures
are sent in a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited
request or falling under some other express exception. Regulation 18901(c)(4) provides
that mailings which contain only information ormaterial sent in response to an
unsolicited specific request are not counted toward the 201 items that equate to a mass
mailing. As used in the regulation, a request is unsolicited if it is not requested or
induced by the elected officer or any other person acting at his or her behest. (Regulation
18901(c)(aXA).) This would apply to copies picked up at District offices or passed out at
meetings, and would include copies sent to persons who make an unsolicited specific
request for the material (such as subscribers).
Exceptions
While regulation 18901 provides a varietyof exceptions to theprohibition, none
appear to fit the brochure described in your letter.
Assuming all four of the criteria established in regulation 18901(a) are met,
publication and diskibution of the brochure as described in your letter would be
prohibited. A copy ofregulation 18901 is enclosed for your convenience.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
CrU.* jJ,^tl,-t
By:
Carla Wardlow
Chiel Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure

formal written advice. (Regulation 1 8329(c).)
